title: Packing List
text:
Luggage Strategy

Checked Bag
1 checked bag per person (max 50 lbs/23 kg for economy)
What Goes Here: - Most clothes - Toiletries over 3.4 oz - Shoes (except what you’re wearing) - Souvenirs on return


Carry-On
1 carry-on + 1 personal item
Carry-On Bag Contains: - 1-2 changes of clothes (in case checked bag delayed) - Medications - Valuables (jewelry, electronics) - Travel documents - Phone chargers - Essentials for first 24 hours
Personal Item (Backpack/Purse): - Passport, wallet, phone - Snacks, water bottle (empty through security) - Book, headphones - Sweater/light jacket

title: Packing List
text:
Clothing

Tops (Pack 5-7)

3-4 Short-sleeve shirts (breathable fabrics)
1-2 Long-sleeve shirts (sun protection, churches)
1 Light cardigan or jacket (AC in museums, trains)
1 Nicer top for dinners

Fabrics: Linen, cotton, moisture-wicking (avoid synthetic in heat)


Bottoms (Pack 3-4)

2 Pairs comfortable pants/jeans (NOT too tight or low-cut for churches)
1 Pair shorts (knee-length for church dress code)
1 Skirt or dress (if preferred)

Church Dress Code: Shoulders and knees covered (carry scarf to cover up)


Dresses/Skirts (Optional, 2-3)

Sundresses (with scarf to cover shoulders in churches)
Casual skirt (knee-length or longer)



Outerwear

1 Light rain jacket (packable, also windbreaker)
1 Sweater or light jacket for AC



Undergarments & Sleepwear

7-8 Underwear
2-3 Bras (including 1 sports bra for walking)
7-8 Pairs socks (light, moisture-wicking)
Pajamas/sleepwear



Accessories

Sun hat or cap (wide brim for sun protection)
Sunglasses
Scarf (covers shoulders for churches, stylish, warmth on plane)
Belt
Swimsuit (hotel pools, maybe Puglia beaches)

title: Packing List
text:
Footwear (Most Important!)

Walking Shoes (Pack 2 Pairs)
Priority #1: You’ll walk 5-10 miles per day on cobblestones
Option 1: Broken-in sneakers or walking shoes - MUST be already broken in (no blisters!) - Good arch support - Cushioned sole - Examples: Allbirds, Hoka, Brooks, New Balance
Option 2: Comfortable sandals with support - NOT flip-flops (no support) - Closed-toe for cobblestones - Examples: Birkenstocks, Teva, Keen
Rule: Alternate shoes daily (give feet/shoes rest)


Nicer Shoes (1 Pair)

Comfortable flats or low heels for dinners
MUST still be walkable (you’ll walk to restaurant)
Avoid stilettos on cobblestones

Pro Tip: Break in ALL shoes 2 months before trip!

title: Packing List
text:
Toiletries

TSA-Compliant (3.4 oz / 100ml for carry-on)

Toothbrush & toothpaste
Deodorant
Shampoo & conditioner (or buy there)
Face wash
Moisturizer (with SPF for daytime)
Razor
Contact lens solution (if applicable)
Makeup (minimal - it’s hot)



Sun Protection (Essential!)

Sunscreen SPF 30+ (apply daily, reapply)
Lip balm with SPF
After-sun lotion (aloe)



Medications

Prescription meds (2-week supply + extra)
Pain reliever (ibuprofen/acetaminophen)
Antihistamine (allergies)
Anti-diarrheal (just in case)
Band-aids & blister prevention
Any personal medications

Bring in original bottles with prescriptions!


First Aid

Band-aids (for blisters!)
Moleskin or Compeed (blister prevention/treatment)
Antibiotic ointment
Tweezers
Pain relief patches

title: Packing List
text:
Electronics

Essential

Phone (unlocked for international SIM or with int’l plan)
Phone charger
Portable battery pack
Power adapter (Type C, F, or L plugs for Italy)
Headphones
Camera (if not using phone)
E-reader or tablet



Optional

Laptop (if needed for work)
Universal power strip (charge multiple devices)
Selfie stick (if you use)

Note: Most hotels have 1-2 outlets. Bring power strip if multiple devices.

title: Packing List
text:
Travel Documents

Must Have (Carry-On Only!)

Passport (valid 6+ months beyond return date)
Printed flight confirmations
Printed hotel confirmations
Printed train tickets / museum reservations
Travel insurance documents
Credit cards (2-3, notify banks of travel dates)
Debit card (ATM withdrawals for cash)
Driver’s license (as backup ID)
Emergency contact list
Copy of itinerary

Pro Tip: Email yourself scans of passport, credit cards, confirmations as backup.

title: Packing List
text:
Money

Cash

€200-300 in small bills (€10, €20, €50)
Get from bank before leaving or ATM at airport
Avoid currency exchange kiosks (high fees)



Cards

2-3 Credit cards (Visa/Mastercard widely accepted)
1 Debit card (ATM withdrawals)
Notify banks of travel dates to avoid fraud holds

Tip: Many Italian establishments cash-only or €10 minimum for cards

title: Packing List
text:
Bags & Organization

Day Bag

Crossbody bag or backpack (hands-free, anti-theft)
Must fit: wallet, phone, water, sunscreen, guidebook
Zippers (not magnets) for pickpocket protection

Recommended: Anti-theft travel purse (slash-proof, RFID blocking)


Packing Cubes

Packing cubes (organize clothes, compress)
Laundry bag (dirty clothes)
Shoe bags



Plastic Bags

Gallon Ziplocs (wet clothes, toiletries, leaky items)
Grocery bags (dirty shoes, wet swimsuit)

title: Packing List
text:
Miscellaneous

Helpful Items

Reusable water bottle (refill at fountains)
Small umbrella (June thunderstorms)
Earplugs (noisy hotels, especially Naples)
Eye mask (for sleeping)
Travel pillow (for planes/trains)
Guidebook or maps (downloaded offline)
Small notebook & pen
Tissues/hand wipes
Hand sanitizer
Snacks (granola bars for long train rides)
Laundry detergent packets (sink washing)
Clothesline or clips (hang dry clothes)



Optional But Nice

Binoculars (for art details, views)
Foldable tote bag (for market purchases, souvenirs)
Italian phrasebook or app
Travel journal
Playing cards

title: Packing List
text:
What NOT to Pack

Leave at Home

❌ Hairdryer (hotels provide, heavy)
❌ Full-size toiletries (buy there or use travel size)
❌ Too many shoes (max 3 pairs)
❌ Formal clothes (not needed unless specific event)
❌ Heavy books (use e-reader)
❌ Expensive jewelry (risk of loss/theft)
❌ Too many “just in case” items

Rule of Thumb: If unsure, leave it. You can buy almost anything in Italy.

title: Packing List
text:
Packing Strategy

2 Weeks Before

Lay out everything you think you’ll bring
Try on all clothes (make sure they fit, comfortable)
Break in new shoes
Test all electronics (charge, ensure working)



1 Week Before

Remove 1/3 of what you laid out (you packed too much)
Check weather forecast
Do laundry of items you’re bringing
Gather toiletries



3 Days Before

Pack non-essentials in checked bag
Make sure medications are accessible
Charge all electronics
Print documents



Day Before

Pack carry-on
Weigh bags (checked max 50 lbs)
Set aside travel outfit
Double-check passport, tickets, confirmations



Day Of

Wear heaviest shoes (save weight in luggage)
Layer clothes on plane (adjust to temperature)
Keep valuables in personal item

title: Packing List
text:
Special Considerations

Church Dress Code
Required: Shoulders and knees covered
Solutions: - Carry lightweight scarf (covers shoulders instantly) - Wear knee-length shorts/skirt - Long pants always acceptable - Some churches provide wraps (but not all)
They WILL turn you away if improperly dressed at major churches/museums.


Walking on Cobblestones

Broken-in shoes with support
Avoid thin soles, high heels, flip-flops
Bring moleskin for blisters
Expect sore feet - it’s normal!



Heat Management

Light colors (reflect sun)
Breathable fabrics (linen, cotton)
Sunscreen (SPF 30+ daily)
Hat and sunglasses
Refillable water bottle

title: Packing List
text:
Final Checklist

3 Days Before Departure

Check passport validity
Print all confirmations
Notify credit card companies
Download offline maps
Pack medications
Charge all devices
Set up international phone plan or buy SIM



Day of Departure

Passport in carry-on
Boarding pass (digital or printed)
Empty water bottle (refill after security)
Snacks for plane
Entertainment (book, music, podcast)
Comfortable travel outfit


Buon viaggio! Pack light, walk far, eat well. 🇮🇹

title: Organized Tours

While most of our Italy trip is self-guided, we’re planning two organized tour experiences:

Herculaneum Archaeological Tour (June 11) - Confirmed priority
Puglia Regional Tour (June 14-19) - Details being finalized

title: Organized Tours
text:
Herculaneum Archaeological Tour (June 11)

Why Herculaneum?
Herculaneum is the more intimate, better-preserved, and less crowded alternative to Pompeii. While Pompeii was buried under ash, Herculaneum was sealed under a 20-meter lava flow, which preserved:

Upper floors of buildings
Wooden furniture and architectural elements
Carbonized food
Intact frescoes
Even organic materials like fabrics

Most Powerful Moment: The Boat Houses, where 300 victims were discovered huddled together. Skeletons are still visible - the most emotionally powerful archaeological site in Italy.


Getting to Herculaneum
From Naples: - Circumvesuviana train from Napoli Centrale (Garibaldi station, lower level) - Direction: Sorrento - Exit at: Ercolano Scavi station - Duration: ~20 minutes - Price: ~€2.80 - Then: 10-minute downhill walk to entrance
Note: Tours often include transportation from Naples hotel, eliminating need to navigate Circumvesuviana.


What You’ll See at Herculaneum
The Boat Houses - 300 victims discovered huddled together seeking escape by sea - Skeletons still visible in original positions - Most emotionally powerful moment in archaeology - Understanding of the eruption’s human toll
House of Neptune and Amphitrite - One of the most vivid Roman mosaics in existence - Brilliant blue and gold glass tesserae - Perfectly preserved dining area (triclinium) - Shows wealth and taste of Roman elite
Casa dei Cervi (House of the Stags) - Elite villa with spectacular frescoes - Garden and former sea-view terrace - Marble sculptures of stags being attacked by dogs - Multi-level structure showing urban Roman architecture
The Thermopolium - Ancient Roman “fast food” counter - Preserved terracotta jars (dolia) for food storage - Shows daily life of common Romans - Still has the marble counter and serving area
Sacello degli Augustali - Spectacular frescoes of Hercules (city’s legendary founder) - Priests’ building with elaborate decoration - Best-preserved Roman wall paintings
Preserved Organic Materials - Wooden doors, beds, shelving - Baby’s cradle - Rope - Food (carbonized bread, nuts, dates) - Items completely destroyed at Pompeii


Tour Options
A guided tour is strongly recommended. The difference between wandering alone and having an archaeologist explain the details is enormous.


Option 1: Small Group - Askos Tours (Best Value)
Duration: 2 hours Group Size: Max 20 people (often smaller in practice) Guide: Licensed archaeologist Included: Skip-the-line tickets + guided tour Price: ~€50-€65 per person Rating: 4.5/5 on Viator (1,160+ reviews)
Booking: Viator.com - Search “Herculaneum Small Group Tour”
Pros: - Excellent value - Professional archaeologist guides - Skip ticket lines - Small enough for personal attention - Highly rated
Cons: - Fixed departure time - Shared with other travelers - Limited ability to linger at specific areas



Option 2: Private Archaeologist - Askos Tours
Duration: 2 hours, at your pace Group Size: Private (just our party) Guide: Licensed archaeologist Included: Skip-the-line tickets + personalized tour Price: ~€180-€250 total (not per person) Booking: AskOsTours.com/herculaneum
Pros: - Go at your own pace - Ask unlimited questions - Customize focus areas - Spend more/less time as desired - Excellent value for 2+ people
Cons: - Higher cost for solo travelers - Need to coordinate meeting time
Recommendation: This is our top choice for personalized experience without high cost.



Option 3: Private Both Sites - LivTours (Gold Standard)
Duration: 6-7 hours (full day) Included: Both Pompeii + Herculaneum, private chauffeur transfer, all tickets Guide: Expert archaeologist Transportation: Private driver between sites and from/to Naples hotel Price: ~€400-€600 total for 2 people Rating: 4.8/5 across platforms Booking: LivTours.com
Pros: - See both major sites in one day - No logistics worries (driver handles everything) - VIP experience - Comprehensive archaeological day - Hotel pickup/drop-off
Cons: - Full day commitment (can be exhausting) - Higher cost - Pompeii can be overwhelming in summer heat - May feel rushed if trying to see both thoroughly
Best For: Those who want to see both Pompeii and Herculaneum and prefer hassle-free logistics.



Option 4: Full Day Private Tour with Vesuvius
Duration: 8 hours Included: Hotel pickup, private driver, Herculaneum + Vesuvius crater hike + Pompeii (optional) Entrance Fees: Included Guide: Can upgrade to include archaeologist throughout or just at ruins Price: ~€350-€550 total for 2 people (guide upgrade extra) Booking: Viator.com - Search “Herculaneum Vesuvius Pompeii Private”
Itinerary: - Morning: Herculaneum ruins (2 hours) - Midday: Drive up Mt. Vesuvius, hike to crater rim (1-1.5 hours) - Afternoon: Optional Pompeii or return to Naples
Pros: - Combines archaeology with volcano experience - See the source of the destruction - Incredible views from Vesuvius crater - Flexible itinerary
Cons: - Long day - Vesuvius hike requires moderate fitness (steep path) - Can’t go deep into all sites if doing all three
Best For: Those who want to understand the full Vesuvius story and are physically able to hike.




Our Recommendation
For Herculaneum Only: Askos Private Tour (Option 2) - €180-250 for personalized 2-hour experience with archaeologist
If Adding Vesuvius: Full Day Private (Option 4) - €350-550 for Herculaneum + Vesuvius crater hike
If Seeing Both Ancient Sites: LivTours (Option 3) - €400-600 for comprehensive Pompeii + Herculaneum with all logistics
Budget Option: Askos Small Group (Option 1) - €50-65 per person for excellent guided experience



Booking Checklist

Decide on tour option (Herculaneum only vs. combo)
Book 2-4 weeks in advance (tours fill up)
Confirm meeting point (hotel pickup vs. meet at site)
Note guide’s name and contact number
Add to calendar with confirmation number
Plan appropriate clothing (comfortable shoes, sun protection)
Bring water bottle
Charge camera/phone



What to Bring
Essential: - Comfortable walking shoes (uneven ancient streets) - Sun protection (hat, sunglasses, sunscreen) - Water bottle (stays hydrated in June heat) - Camera with charged battery
Recommended: - Small backpack or crossbody bag - Cash for tips (€5-10 for guide, optional but appreciated) - Notebook if you want to take notes - Light layers (some buildings cooler than outside)
Not Needed: - Large bags (bag check available but cumbersome) - Umbrellas (ruins partially exposed to elements)

title: Organized Tours
text:
Puglia Regional Tour (June 14-19)

Status: Tour Being Finalized
We’re currently planning a 6-day organized tour of Puglia from June 14-19, 2026. This section will be updated once tour details are confirmed.


What We Know
Dates: June 14-19, 2026 (6 days / 5 nights) Region: Puglia (southeastern Italy, the “heel of the boot”) Starting Point: Brindisi (arriving June 13 from Naples) Tour Type: Organized group or semi-private tour Focus: Culture, architecture, food, wine, coastal beauty


Expected Highlights
Based on typical Puglia tours, we expect to visit:
Alberobello - UNESCO World Heritage trulli houses - Unique cone-shaped stone buildings - Trulli wine tasting - Overnight stay possible
Lecce - “Florence of the South” - Baroque architecture in golden limestone - Piazza del Duomo - Roman amphitheater - Artisan workshops
Polignano a Mare - Dramatic cliffside coastal town - Beaches and sea caves - Red Bull Cliff Diving location - Fresh seafood restaurants
Ostuni - La Città Bianca (“The White City”) - Hilltop old town with whitewashed buildings - Panoramic views over olive groves - Medieval character
Other Possible Stops: - Matera (technically Basilicata, but often included) - Ancient cave dwellings - Castel del Monte - Frederick II’s mysterious octagonal castle - Otranto - Easternmost town in Italy, beautiful cathedral - Locorotondo - Circular hilltop town - Cisternino - Another white hilltop town - Coastal areas and beaches


Puglia Experiences
Culinary: - Orecchiette pasta (shaped like “little ears”) - Burrata cheese (originated in Puglia) - Focaccia Barese - Fresh seafood - Primitivo and Negroamaro wines - Olive oil tastings (Puglia produces 40% of Italy’s olive oil)
Cultural: - Trullo architecture (unique to Puglia) - Baroque churches and piazzas - Masseria (fortified farmhouse) stays - Traditional folk music and dance - Artisan workshops (ceramics, paper mache)


Tour Logistics (To Be Confirmed)
Accommodation: - Likely included in tour package - May rotate between towns or stay in one base - Possible masseria (traditional farmhouse) experience
Meals: - Some meals likely included - Emphasis on regional cuisine - Wine tastings probable
Transportation: - Tour bus or van - All inter-city transport within Puglia included - Hotel to hotel transfers


What We Need to Finalize

Tour operator selection
Exact daily itinerary
Accommodation details (which towns, which hotels)
Meal plan (which meals included)
Pricing and deposit
Group size (small group vs. private)
Free time vs. structured activities
Cancellation policy



Update Timeline
We expect to finalize Puglia tour details by late March/early April 2026. This page will be updated with:

Complete daily itinerary
Hotel information
Tour operator details
Specific activities and visits
Booking confirmation

See Also: Puglia Regional Overview for more information about the region.

title: Organized Tours
text:
Tour Booking Tips (General)

When to Book
Herculaneum Tours: - Book 2-4 weeks in advance - Popular times (summer weekends) book faster - Weekday mornings less crowded
Puglia Tours: - Book 2-3 months in advance - Multi-day tours require more planning - Summer is peak season


What to Ask Tour Operators

Group size (smaller is better for archaeology sites)
Guide credentials (archaeologist vs. general guide)
Cancellation policy
What’s included (tickets, transportation, meals)
Meeting point and time
Physical difficulty level
Language (English-speaking guide confirmed?)



Red Flags to Avoid

Extremely cheap prices (often crowded mega-buses)
Vague itineraries
No reviews or very few reviews
High-pressure booking tactics
No clear cancellation policy



Payment & Confirmation

Book through reputable platforms (Viator, TripAdvisor, direct with operator)
Get written confirmation email
Save booking number and emergency contact
Check credit card for proper charge
Screenshot confirmation for offline access
